description.p: LinSight is a fast, beautiful, modular Linux system-monitoring dashboard. It shows live CPU, RAM, multi-GPU (NVIDIA + Intel xe), NVMe, and network sensors in a single Overview page, with a runtime plugin system for hardware that isn't covered out of the box.
Key features:

ul.li: Subscription-driven sampling — sensors only run when something is watching
Built-in support for NVIDIA NVML and Intel xe driver GPUs
Per-NVMe SSD temperatures and bytes-read/written counters
Per-interface network rx/tx counters and link state
Always-on mode (opt-in): SQLite history + Prometheus exporter
Drop-in .so plugin support for new hardware

description.p: Repo hygiene: stopped tracking a machine-specific .qmlls.ini that pinned an absolute local build path; it is now git-ignored. No change to the shipped binary.
Security: .gitignore now excludes mTLS key/cert material so a locally generated linsight-tunnel keypair cannot be committed by accident.

Fix: clicking "Open" on a dashboard in the Gallery did nothing when it was the dashboard you had just left — the Gallery button only cleared the view slug without resetting the page-stack key, so a subsequent "Open" of that same dashboard was short-circuited. The Gallery button now resets the page key, so "Open" always navigates.
The sidebar tagline now reads "Whole system insight" instead of the outdated "Multi-GPU system insight".

Fix: GUI memory could grow without bound when QML fell behind live samples. Live sample updates now coalesce before crossing into Qt, so a slow render path keeps only the latest pending frame instead of accumulating queued JSON update closures.

Fix: the GUI auto-reconnects to the daemon instead of getting stuck on "Disconnected". A keepalive stops the daemon's 30-minute idle timeout from evicting a live-but-quiet dashboard, and a connection supervisor automatically reconnects (respawning the local daemon if needed) whenever the connection drops.

Packaging release: rebuilds the AppImage so the GPU, storage, and dashboard tile-rendering fixes from 1.19.0 are carried in it, verified to render correctly under the AppImage's bundled Qt 6.4. No source changes since 1.19.0.

Fix: GPU and storage tiles frozen on "…" across category pages and dashboards. The Qt pages merged each tick's value delta by mutating a var map/array in place and reassigning the same object reference, which QML's identity-based change detection ignores, so the value bindings never re-evaluated. Fixed on the GPUs and Storage pages, custom dashboards, the dashboard editor, and the Overview sparklines.

Fix: static sensor values (GPU VRAM, total RAM, disk capacity) could stay on "…". A static sensor is sampled once then parked, so its value arrives in a single delta a late-loading page could miss; the GUI now re-emits already-sampled static tiles in every delta so any page converges.

Fix: static sensors were parked after the first scheduler tick even when their sample was dropped by the bounded sampler at cold start. Parking now happens on a successful sample only, so a dropped or errored static reading retries on the next due tick instead of hanging on "…" forever.

Dependency maintenance: upgrade the workspace crate tree (stabby 72, rusqlite 0.40, toml 1.1, evalexpr 13, rcgen 0.14, criterion 0.8, and others). evalexpr relicensed to AGPL-3.0-only, permitted in a GPL-3.0 project via GPLv3 section 13. Regenerate and reconcile the third-party license notices and credits.

Fix the packaged GUI failing to launch (no window): QML pages imported shared JS helpers via an absolute qrc:/qml/Shared.js URL the older Qt bundled in the AppImage could not resolve. Import them relatively so they load on every Qt 6.x.

Performance and UI polish: Arc-shared samples and catalogues, reusable serialization buffers, dirty-delta GUI tile updates, lightweight history records, shared alert context, stack-buffer sysfs reads, dashboard import in Rust, mutable daemon settings toggles, and CLI format validation.

Harden daemon and GUI against hangs and leaks: capped, time-boxed sensor worker threads, async alert notifications, transport and client socket timeouts, idempotent subscriptions, and tunnel/Prometheus write-timeouts.
Benchmarks, property tests, daemon transport unit tests, GUI smoke in CI, and plugin sandbox design groundwork.

Bugfix release: fixed a GitHub release race that could drop AppImage and Flatpak artifacts; added GUI client dispatch tests; refactored daemon XDG path helpers; and added a deterministic wire-size budget guard.

UI polish batch: dedicated Network page with per-interface throughput, persisted process-table state, copy sensor ID from any tile, read-only Prometheus bind hint in Settings, and refreshed translations.
Multi-host view: save remote hosts, switch between them from the sidebar, and survive reconnects with automatic catalogue rebuild, subscription replay, and pump-interval replay.
Version bump to 1.11.0.

Process explorer page with sortable, filterable process table. SMART disk health sensors via udisks2. Alert event log with per-rule cooldown. Sensor snapshot caches for disk, net, fs, and system plugins. History charts with configurable retention pruning.

Hardening and performance update: client subscriptions are isolated and cleaned up on disconnect, history and Prometheus paths are bounded, CPU and memory sensors reuse short-lived snapshots, dashboard writes are more race-resistant, GUI daemon RPCs no longer block the QML thread, and CLI/tunnel behavior is aligned with the documented defaults.

New application icon: the final LinSight mark (a layered hardware stack with a wavelength "L" and a thermometer accent) replaces the placeholder across all icon sizes and the in-app About and Credits pages. The in-app third-party license credits now list only genuine dependencies, and the documentation set was cleaned up.

Identity update: the application ID is now com.visorcraft.LinSight (matching the visorcraft.com domain), changed across the desktop entry, AppStream metadata, icons, Flatpak app-id and the window id. Users with a pinned launcher should re-pin LinSight after upgrading.
Maintenance update: the bundled third-party license notices were renamed to third-party-notices.md and regenerated against the current dependency set, and the translation catalogs were refreshed.

Packaging and release-automation update: continuous integration plus one-command multi-format release builds (portable tarball, Arch, Debian, Fedora, openSUSE, AppImage and Flatpak), with assorted packaging fixes and launcher icons in the distro packages.

Security and robustness hardening: a panicking plugin is now isolated from the daemon instead of crashing it (plugin ABI v6); the remote tunnel's idle timeout no longer drops healthy long-lived sessions; and webhook alert targets are validated against SSRF. Out-of-tree plugins must be rebuilt against the v6 SDK.

Storage page nests mount points inside their physical disk as collapsible cards; GPU sections ordered by VRAM with unified "GPU VRAM" sensor naming; inode sensors skipped for filesystems that do not report them.

All 10 spec phases shipped: daemon, CLI, Qt 6 / Kirigami GUI with sidebar, preset pages (Overview, GPUs, Storage, Network), canvas editor, alerts engine, Prometheus exporter, SQLite history, runtime .so plugin ABI v2 (stabby R-mirror types), and the linsight-tunnel mTLS bridge.
Initial preview — Phase 1 through Phase 10 scaffolding.
